steps.args: {'prompt': "From Zillow's 2024 10-K, extract: (1) revenue by category/segment for 2024 and 2023, including Residential, Rentals, Mortgages and any subcategories such as Premier Agent, new construction, ShowingTime+, Follow Up Boss, multifamily, etc.; (2) definitions of Residential revenue and whether Follow Up Boss is included; (3) any discussion of revenue mix, growth areas, strategy/super app, acquisition strategy or acquisitions closed in 2023/2024. Provide exact figures and citations/quotes. {{zillow_2024_10k}}"}
steps.result:
Below are the requested extracts from Zillow Group’s 2024 Form 10‑K. Dollar amounts are in **millions** unless noted.

## 1) Revenue by category / “segment” for 2024 and 2023

### Reportable segment context

Zillow does **not** report multiple operating segments in 2024. It reports one segment:

> “Our chief executive officer, who acts as the chief operating decision maker, makes operating decisions and evaluates operating performance on the basis of the company as a whole. Accordingly, we operate as a single reportable segment.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

Note 18 similarly states:

> “As a result, we have determined that we have a single reportable segment.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Note 18, “Segment Information”

### Revenue table — 2024 vs. 2023

Zillow’s revenue categories are Residential, Mortgages, Rentals and Other. It also presents **“For Sale revenue”** as Residential + Mortgages.

> “Our ‘For Sale revenue’ subtotal includes our Residential and Mortgages revenue categories and represents our revenue from participation in residential real estate purchase and sale transactions.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Revenue Overview”

| Revenue category | 2024 | % of total 2024 | 2023 | % of total 2023 | $ change | % change |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Residential | $1,594 | 71% | $1,452 | 75% | $142 | 10% |
| Mortgages | 145 | 6% | 96 | 5% | 49 | 51% |
| **Total For Sale revenue** | **1,739** | **78%** | **1,548** | **80%** | **191** | **12%** |
| Rentals | 453 | 20% | 357 | 18% | 96 | 27% |
| Other | 44 | 2% | 40 | 2% | 4 | 10% |
| **Total revenue** | **$2,236** | **100%** | **$1,945** | **100%** | **$291** | **15%** |

Source: 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Results of Operations — Revenue.” The same dollar amounts are repeated in Note 17:

> “The following table presents our revenue disaggregated by category…” with 2024 revenue of Residential $1,594; Mortgages $145; Rentals $453; Other $44; Total revenue $2,236; and 2023 revenue of Residential $1,452; Mortgages $96; Rentals $357; Other $40; Total revenue $1,945.  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Note 17, “Revenue and Contract Balances”

### Subcategory disclosure

The 10‑K **does not disclose separate dollar revenue** for Premier Agent, new construction, ShowingTime+, Follow Up Boss, multifamily rentals, Zillow Showcase, StreetEasy Experts, etc. It only discloses dollars at the four revenue-category level above, plus qualitative / percentage drivers for certain subcategories.

## 2) Definitions of Residential revenue and whether Follow Up Boss is included

### Residential revenue definition

Zillow defines Residential revenue as follows:

> “Residential revenue includes revenue generated by our Premier Agent and new construction marketplaces, as well as revenue from the sale of advertising and business technology solutions for real estate professionals through ShowingTime+, StreetEasy for-sale product offerings and, upon acquisition on December 8, 2023, Follow Up Boss.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Revenue Overview”

Note 2 gives the same definition:

> “Residential revenue includes revenue generated by our Premier Agent and new construction marketplaces, as well as revenue from the sale of advertising and business technology solutions for real estate professionals through ShowingTime+, StreetEasy for-sale product offerings, and Follow Up Boss.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Note 2, “Revenue Recognition — Residential”

### Is Follow Up Boss included?

Yes. Follow Up Boss is explicitly included in Residential revenue beginning with its acquisition date:

> “Residential revenue includes… Follow Up Boss.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Note 2, “Revenue Recognition — Residential”

And Item 7 is even more explicit:

> “…and, upon acquisition on December 8, 2023, Follow Up Boss.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Revenue Overview”

### Residential subcategories described in the 10‑K

| Residential subcategory | 10‑K description / quote |
|---|---|
| Premier Agent | “Premier Agent revenue is generated by the sale of advertising services, as well as marketing and technology products and services, to help real estate agents and brokers grow and manage their businesses and brands.” — Item 7, “Revenue Overview” |
| New construction | “New construction revenue primarily includes advertising services sold to home builders on a cost per residential community or cost per impression basis.” — Item 7, “Revenue Overview” |
| ShowingTime+ | “Revenue generated through ShowingTime+ includes ShowingTime revenue, which is primarily generated by Appointment Center… ShowingTime+ revenue also includes our dotloop real estate transaction management SaaS solution, as well as Zillow Showcase, a listing marketing package.” — Item 7, “Revenue Overview” |
| StreetEasy for-sale | “StreetEasy for-sale revenue primarily consists of our StreetEasy Experts and StreetEasy subscription offerings.” — Item 7, “Revenue Overview” |
| Follow Up Boss | “Follow Up Boss revenue is generated through our SaaS customer relationship management system which provides real estate agents, teams and brokerages with a central hub to manage real estate transactions from connection to close.” — Item 7, “Revenue Overview” |

## 3) Other revenue category definitions

### Mortgages

> “Mortgages revenue primarily includes revenue generated through mortgage originations and the related sale of mortgages on the secondary market through Zillow Home Loans and revenue from advertising sold to mortgage lenders and other mortgage professionals on a cost per lead basis through our Custom Quote and Connect services.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Revenue Overview”

### Rentals

> “Rentals revenue includes advertising and a suite of tools sold to property managers on a cost per lead, lease, listing or impression basis or for a fixed fee for certain advertising packages through both the Zillow and StreetEasy brands. Rentals revenue also includes revenue generated from our rental applications product…”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Revenue Overview”

### Other

> “Other revenue includes revenue generated primarily by display advertising.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Revenue Overview”

## 4) Revenue mix and growth discussion

### Revenue mix

Zillow’s 2024 revenue remained primarily Residential / For Sale, but Rentals grew as a share of revenue.

* Residential: **71% of 2024 revenue**, down from **75% in 2023**
* Mortgages: **6%**, up from **5%**
* Total For Sale: **78%**, down from **80%**
* Rentals: **20%**, up from **18%**
* Other: **2%**, unchanged

Source: 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Results of Operations — Revenue.”

### Residential growth

Zillow attributed Residential growth to higher revenue per visit, more visits, Follow Up Boss, Premier Agent conversion, ShowingTime+ and new construction:

> “Residential revenue increased $142 million, or 10%. The increase in Residential revenue was partially driven by a 6% increase in Residential revenue per visit to $0.171… primarily due to the inclusion of revenue from Follow Up Boss, which we acquired in December 2023, growth in our Premier Agent revenue primarily due to continued improvement in our ability to convert connections into transactions, an increase in ShowingTime+ revenue as we expanded software services available to sellers and listing agents, and continued growth in our new construction revenue.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Results of Operations — Revenue”

### Mortgages growth

Mortgages revenue increased sharply, driven by Zillow Home Loans origination volume:

> “Mortgages revenue increased $49 million, or 51%. This increase was driven by a $64 million increase in mortgage originations revenue, partially offset by a $12 million decrease in Custom Quote and Connect advertising services revenue.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Results of Operations — Revenue”

Loan origination volume:

| Zillow Home Loans origination volume | 2024 | 2023 | % change |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Purchase loan origination volume | $3,092 | $1,534 | 102% |
| Refinance loan origination volume | 27 | 16 | 69% |
| **Total loan origination volume** | **$3,119** | **$1,550** | **101%** |

Source: 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Key Metrics — Loan Origination Volume.”

### Rentals growth and multifamily

Rentals growth was driven by higher monetization, more rentals visitors and multifamily listings:

> “Rentals revenue increased $96 million, or 27%. The increase in Rentals revenue was primarily due to a 15% increase in quarterly revenue per average monthly rentals unique visitor to $3.65… as compared to $3.19… primarily driven by growth in multifamily property listings, which drove a 42% increase in multifamily rentals revenue.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Results of Operations — Revenue”

Zillow also stated:

> “The increase in Rentals revenue was also driven by growth in average monthly rentals unique visitors which increased 11% to 31 million during the year ended December 31, 2024, from 28 million during the year ended December 31, 2023.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Results of Operations — Revenue”

## 5) Strategy / “super app” / growth areas

### Housing super app strategy

Zillow frames its strategy as a “housing super app”:

> “We are helping renters, buyers, sellers, and real estate professionals across all their residential real estate needs through our ‘housing super app’ which serves as an ecosystem of connected solutions for the tasks and services related to moving.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

It further states:

> “We are focused on continually improving our consumer funnel, capturing consumer demand and connecting that demand to our partner network.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

### Enhanced Markets / integrated transaction strategy

Zillow describes Enhanced Markets as central to its For Sale strategy:

> “Enhanced Markets are geographies in which consumers can easily navigate the home-buying process digitally and seamlessly. In these markets, consumers benefit from a more streamlined experience from touring to closing, with the support of our agent partners and Zillow Home Loans loan officers.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

As of year-end 2024:

> “As of December 31, 2024, we are active in 43 Enhanced Markets, which represents 21% of our total connections, or leads delivered to our Premier Agent partners.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

Zillow ties this to For Sale revenue performance:

> “Our increased For Sale revenue per TTV is an indicator that our investments have driven outperformance relative to the industry in 2024.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

For Sale revenue per TTV:

| Metric | 2024 | 2023 | % change |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| For Sale revenue | $1,739 | $1,548 | 12% |
| Total transaction value | $1.7 trillion | $1.6 trillion | 6% |
| For Sale revenue per TTV | 10.1 bps | 9.6 bps | 5% |

Source: 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 7, “Key Metrics — For Sale Revenue Per Total Transaction Value.”

### Industry software growth area

Zillow identifies Industry Software as an investment area and directly connects it to Aryeo and Follow Up Boss:

> “We are investing in digital solutions to empower real estate professionals to drive a better consumer experience for movers.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

> “As examples, we acquired Aryeo and Follow Up Boss, which provide real estate professionals tools to manage leads, create listings, and effectuate transactions.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

Follow Up Boss usage in Enhanced Markets:

> “As of December 31, 2024, more than 80% of Enhanced Market connections are being managed through Follow Up Boss.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Customer Offerings — For Real Estate Professionals”

### Rentals marketplace strategy

Zillow describes Rentals as a major marketplace build-out:

> “We are building a nationwide marketplace for renters and landlords, which aims to provide renters with a comprehensive listing of available rental inventory.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Business — Overview”

It also highlights the 2024 Realtor.com partnership:

> “In 2024, we entered into a strategic partnership with Realtor.com to provide all multifamily listings on their site, and we launched our first national marketing campaign for Zillow Rentals dedicated to apartment seekers.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1, “Customer Offerings — For Renters”

## 6) Acquisition strategy and acquisitions closed in 2023 / 2024

### Acquisition strategy

Zillow says it continues to evaluate strategic opportunities:

> “We continue to evaluate a wide array of potential strategic opportunities, including acquisitions and investments.”  
> — 2024 Form 10‑K, Item 1A, “Risk Factors — We have and may continue to make acquisitions and investments…”

### Acquisitions closed / discussed

| Acquisition | Date | Consideration / figures disclosed | 10‑K citation / quote |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Aryeo | July 31, 2023 | Approx. $15 million cash, net of cash acquired, plus 380,259 Class C shares valued at $20 million; total consideration $35 million net of cash acquired | “On July 31, 2023, Zillow Group acquired Aryeo… in exchange for approximately $15 million in cash, net of cash acquired, and 380,259 shares of our Class C capital stock with a value of $20 million, for total consideration of $35 million, net of cash acquired.” — Note 6 |
| Spruce | September 11, 2023 | Approx. $19 million cash, net of cash acquired | “On September 11, 2023, Zillow Group acquired substantially all of the assets and liabilities of Spruce… in exchange for approximately $19 million in cash, net of cash acquired.” — Note 6 |
| Follow Up Boss | December 8, 2023 | $399 million cash, net of cash acquired, plus contingent consideration up to $100 million; total purchase price allocation $484 million | “On December 8, 2023, Zillow Group acquired Follow Up Boss… for $399 million in cash, net of cash acquired, and contingent consideration of up to $100 million…” — Note 6 |
| Virtual Staging AI Inc. | October 2024 | Separate purchase price not disclosed; 2024 cash flow statement shows “Cash paid for acquisitions, net” of $7 million | Zillow lists it among recent acquisitions: “we acquired… Virtual Staging AI Inc. in October 2024.” — Item 1A, “Risk Factors” |

## Zillow acquisition of Aryeo

**Date / status**
- Announced by Zillow on **August 2, 2023**.
- Cooley also lists Aryeo as one of Zillow Group’s acquisitions in **August 2023**.

**What Aryeo does**
- Aryeo is described as “**a leading real estate media software and content management platform for photographers and media companies**.”
- It was founded in **2019** by former real estate photographers **Branick Weix, Matthew Michalski and Brendan Quinlan**.
- Aryeo’s platform “**powers thousands of photography and media businesses nationwide**,” which in turn help bring “**hundreds of thousands of real estate listings to life each year for agents and consumers**.”

**Strategic fit with ShowingTime+ / agent listing-media strategy**
- Aryeo joins **ShowingTime+**, Zillow Group’s real estate software brand for agents, brokers and MLSs.
- The acquisition is intended to help ShowingTime+ serve not only agents, but also **photographers and media companies** that create listing content.
- Zillow says Aryeo will help ShowingTime+ “**efficiently and seamlessly deliver high-quality, immersive media to agents**,” including:
  - interactive floor plans
  - media-forward listing products
  - richer listing preparation and marketing tools
- It fits with ShowingTime+ products such as **Listing Media Services** and **Listing Showcase**, which Zillow launched to expand adoption of rich media and interactive content in agent listing packages.
- Aryeo will “**continue to operate as an independent platform within ShowingTime+**,” and its privacy policy remains in place.

**Key quotes**
- Zillow: Aryeo’s mission “**makes it a natural fit with ShowingTime+**.”
- Zillow: Bringing Aryeo into ShowingTime+ “**enables the brand to serve photographers and media companies directly and invest in the tools they need to create elevated, immersive home shopping experiences for real estate agents and consumers**.”
- Cynthia Taylor, VP of product and business strategy for ShowingTime+:  
  > “I’m thrilled to welcome Aryeo to the ShowingTime+ team to help us further our mission to offer elevated real estate experiences. We know elevated real estate experiences happen for consumers when the professionals doing the work are empowered with great products that help them deliver these experiences. ShowingTime+ is already doing this for agents, and we're looking forward to empowering photographers and media companies as well.”
- Branick Weix, Aryeo co-founder and CEO:  
  > “When we created Aryeo, our vision was to build the best platform possible to solve photographer and media company pain points and provide easy-to-use tools to help our customers grow. We’re teaming up with ShowingTime+ because we both believe in building technology that makes the industry better, and we will keep innovating and improving our platform for our customers.”

**Consumer / listing-media rationale**
- Zillow says interactive media has become increasingly important because it gives shoppers “**a deep sense of a home**” and helps reduce unnecessary tours.
- Zillow cited that homes on Zillow with an interactive floor plan were:
  - **saved 79% more** than homes without one
  - **shared 72% more** on average
- Zillow also says most sellers are more likely to hire an agent who includes virtual tours and/or interactive floor plans.

---

## Zillow acquisition of Spruce

**Date / status**
- Zillow originally announced an agreement to acquire Spruce on **August 10, 2023**.
- The acquisition was completed and the post was updated on **September 11, 2023**.
- Cooley notes it advised Zillow on the Spruce acquisition in **September 2023**.

**What Spruce does**
- Spruce is described as “**a tech-enabled title and escrow company**.”
- Founded in **2016**, Spruce built technology and operations to power:
  - title insurance
  - coordination
  - escrow
  - recording services
- Zillow says Spruce served “**some of the most innovative real estate companies**” nationwide and combined proprietary software with experienced title and closing professionals.

**Digital closing / housing super app strategy**
- Zillow says improving the real estate transaction requires improving the “**critical closing process**.”
- Zillow and Spruce plan to build “**a digital-powered closing services offering for Zillow customers**.”
- Zillow frames Spruce as a building block for its **housing super app**, intended to connect the fragmented parts of moving into “**one streamlined transaction**.”
- Zillow says it had previously announced it was sunsetting its existing closing services division in favor of exploring a more tech-forward solution.
- The acquisition supports Zillow’s goal of creating a more seamless, end-to-end home-buying experience for both **customers and partners**.

**Customer / revenue alignment**
- The post does not disclose deal price or revenue terms.
- Strategically, Spruce aligns Zillow more directly with the transaction and closing layer of the home-buying process, allowing Zillow to build a customer-facing closing service that could sit inside its broader housing super app experience.
- Zillow emphasizes high-volume operational capability, describing the goal as a “**tech-forward, high volume title company for the future**.”

**Key quotes**
- Zillow:  
  > “Making it easier for people to move requires improvements across the real estate transaction - including the critical closing process.”
- Zillow:  
  > “Over time, Zillow Group and Spruce will build a digital-powered closing services offering for Zillow customers.”
- Matt Daimler, Zillow senior vice president of product:  
  > “Title should never be a blocker to a great transaction and our goal is for our customers to see and feel how much better the real estate transaction can be when they work with Zillow. With Spruce, Zillow is building a tech-forward, high volume title company for the future.”
- Patrick Burns, Spruce CEO and co-founder:  
  > “Real estate transactions often fall short of buyer and seller expectations. Like Zillow, Spruce is driven by the vision of offering an amazing transaction experience to buyers and sellers. We’re excited to join Zillow in the ambitious mission to create the best closing solution for the modern home-buying process.”
- Zillow:  
  > “It will take time to develop and eventually roll out a new closing solution for Zillow customers, but we expect it to be a key component of Zillow’s housing super app, which will connect all the fragmented pieces of moving into one streamlined transaction.”

---

## Zillow acquisition of Follow Up Boss

**Date / status**
- Cooley announced on **November 1, 2023** that Zillow Group had entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Follow Up Boss.
- Zillow’s post was updated on **December 8, 2023** after the acquisition closed.
- Zillow states: “**Zillow Group has acquired Follow Up Boss**.”
- A precise legal closing date is not separately stated beyond the December 8 update.

**Deal price**
- Cooley says Zillow agreed to acquire Follow Up Boss for:
  - **$400 million in initial cash consideration**
  - plus up to **$100 million in potential cash earnout**

**What Follow Up Boss does**
- Follow Up Boss is “**one of real estate’s leading customer relationship management (CRM) systems**.”
- Zillow describes it as a “**central hub**” that helps teams and agents:
  - stay organized
  - engage customers
  - close deals
  - grow teams

**How it supports agents and Zillow’s strategy**
- Zillow frames the acquisition as an investment to help agents “**better serve customers and grow their businesses**.”
- The acquisition supports Zillow’s broader strategy to improve the home buying and selling experience by giving real estate professionals stronger productivity and customer-management tools.
- Zillow says Follow Up Boss will be able to “**invest further in new products and features to help agents supercharge their businesses to exceed the needs of today’s home buyers and sellers**.”

**Independence / open-platform commitments**
- Zillow emphasized that Follow Up Boss will remain independent:
  > “Follow Up Boss remains an independent brand, and will continue serving its client base across the industry, regardless of whether those clients engage with other Zillow Group platforms.”
- Zillow also committed to maintaining third-party CRM choice:
  > “Zillow Group continues to support third-party CRM integrations on the Zillow Premier Agent app, giving agents the option to work in their CRM of choice and avoid duplicate data entry.”
- Follow Up Boss will continue supporting third-party integrations:
  > “Follow Up Boss also continues to support its vast ecosystem of third-party integration partners, enabling agents to keep using their preferred systems seamlessly.”
- Zillow addressed data privacy and ownership:
  > “The customer data that Follow Up Boss subscribers enter into the system is theirs, just as it always has been, and stays within Follow Up Boss — Zillow Group won’t be able to use it unless the Follow Up Boss subscriber gives permission.”

**Key quotes**
- Zillow:  
  > “At Zillow Group, we’re laser-focused on improving — demystifying, streamlining and de-stressing — the experience of buying and selling a home.”
- Zillow:  
  > “This commitment is at the heart of everything we do, and we do it arm-in-arm with real estate professionals on the front lines with buyers and sellers.”
- Zillow:  
  > “We’re excited to announce an investment that will enable agents to better serve customers and grow their businesses.”
- Zillow:  
  > “We know Follow Up Boss is beloved by teams and agents, providing a central hub to stay organized and engage customers, close deals and grow teams.”

---

## Zillow acquisition of Virtual Staging AI

**Date / status**
- Announced by Zillow on **October 8, 2024**.
- The acquisition **closed October 7, 2024**.
- Zillow says the VSAI teams are “**beginning to integrate into Zillow**.”

**What Virtual Staging AI does**
- Virtual Staging AI, or VSAI, provides AI-powered technology that helps:
  - home sellers
  - agents
  - photographers  
  create digitally staged listing images without physical furniture or décor.
- Real estate professionals can upload room photos, choose a room type and furniture style, and generate staged images almost instantly.
- Zillow says the technology creates photos that are “**nearly indistinguishable from traditionally staged listings**.”

**Integration into Zillow**
- VSAI’s team has joined Zillow’s **AI and tech teams**.
- Zillow says virtual staging is currently available through VSAI’s website.
- Zillow plans to offer the technology to agents and photographers through Zillow’s product suite “**in the near future**.”
- Zillow is working to integrate VSAI into **Zillow Showcase**.

**Listing media / AI strategy**
- Zillow frames the acquisition as part of its broader strategy to elevate the listing experience using AI and richer media.
- VSAI adds another interactive listing-media option alongside virtual tours, interactive floor plans and Showcase-style immersive listings.
- Zillow says the acquisition “**underscores Zillow’s commitment to enabling the real estate industry to become more digital, integrated and productive**.”
- Zillow connects the technology to seller preferences: according to Zillow’s 2024 Consumer Housing Trends Report, **71% of sellers** say they are more likely to hire an agent who includes virtual tours and/or interactive floor plans.
- Zillow says integrating VSAI into Showcase will create “**an even more immersive listing experience**” and help buyers get “**the most realistic, accurate sense of a home before they step inside**.”

**Key quotes**
- Zillow:  
  > “Zillow Group has acquired Virtual Staging AI (VSAI), a company whose technology helps home sellers, agents and photographers create digitally staged listing images without the need for physical furniture and decor.”
- Zillow:  
  > “VSAI offers the next generation of listing innovation to elevate the listing experience on Zillow for home shoppers.”
- Zillow:  
  > “Their AI-powered virtual staging technology generates listing photos that are nearly indistinguishable from traditionally staged listings.”
- Zillow:  
  > “Being able to stage spaces and edit the images within seconds eliminates wait time and back-and-forth to implement changes.”
- Zillow:  
  > “The acquisition of VSAI underscores Zillow’s commitment to enabling the real estate industry to become more digital, integrated and productive.”
- Zillow:  
  > “We’re working to integrate VSAI’s technology into Zillow Showcase, creating an even more immersive listing experience to help clients sell their homes by giving buyers the most realistic, accurate sense of a home before they step inside.”
- Zillow:  
  > “Zillow is leading the digital transformation of real estate, and we’re excited that the VSAI team and their impressive technology will now be part of this effort.”

---

## Cooley deal context

Cooley’s November 1, 2023 release provides additional transaction context:

- Cooley advised Zillow Group on the definitive agreement to acquire **Follow Up Boss**.
- Follow Up Boss deal value: **$400 million initial cash consideration** plus up to **$100 million potential cash earnout**.
- Cooley says it previously advised Zillow Group on:
  - **Spruce**, September 2023
  - **Aryeo**, August 2023
  - **VRX Media**, December 2022

This sequence shows Zillow building around several transaction and listing-experience capabilities: listing media production and management, immersive listing presentation, CRM and agent workflow, and digital title/escrow/closing infrastructure.

steps.result:
From Zillow Group’s 2022 Form 10-K, **2022 revenue was not disclosed in the new “Residential / Rentals / Mortgages / Other” format**. Zillow stated that it **planned to begin reporting** those categories in Q1 2023.

### What the 2022 10-K actually reports for 2022 revenue  
Amounts in **millions**:

| 2022 revenue category reported in 2022 10-K | Revenue |
|---|---:|
| Premier Agent | $1,291 |
| Rentals | $274 |
| Other IMT | $274 |
| Mortgages | $119 |
| **Total revenue** | **$1,958** |

### Requested categories: availability in the 2022 10-K

| Requested category | 2022 amount extractable from 2022 10-K? | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| **Residential** | **Not separately disclosed** | Zillow said this category would begin in Q1 2023. It would include Premier Agent plus parts of “Other IMT” and Homes-related title/escrow services. |
| **Mortgages** | **$119 million** | This category is consistent with Zillow’s historical Mortgages segment revenue. |
| **Rentals** | **$274 million** | This category is consistent with Zillow’s historical Rentals revenue. |
| **Other** | **Not separately disclosed under new definition** | The 2022 “Other IMT” amount was $274 million, but that is **not the same** as the future “Other” category, which Zillow said would primarily include display advertising. |
| **Total** | **$1,958 million** | Total 2022 revenue from continuing operations. |

### Definitions / mapping described in the 10-K

Zillow stated that beginning in Q1 2023 it planned to report revenue categories as follows:

- **Residential**: primarily includes revenue from the **Premier Agent** and **new construction** marketplaces, **StreetEasy for-sale** product offerings, **Zillow Closing Services**, and **ShowingTime+** — including ShowingTime, Bridge Interactive, dotloop, and interactive floor plans.
- **Rentals**: consistent with historical rentals revenue.
- **Mortgages**: consistent with historical mortgages revenue, primarily Zillow Home Loans mortgage originations and advertising sold to mortgage lenders/professionals.
- **Other**: primarily includes **display advertising**.

**Bottom line:** The 2022 10-K gives total 2022 revenue of **$1.958 billion**, but it does **not** provide enough detail to precisely recast 2022 revenue into the future “Residential” and “Other” categories.

{'final_result': 'As of December 2024, Zillow’s acquisition strategy over the prior two years was best characterized as **targeted capability-building for an end-to-end “housing super app,” with a heavy bias toward residential for-sale software, agent workflow, rich listing media, and transaction/closing infrastructure**—not a return to capital-intensive home buying.

## 1) What Zillow bought: mostly tuck-ins around the for-sale transaction

| Date | Acquisition | Consideration disclosed | What Zillow was buying | Strategic purpose |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| July 31, 2023 | **Aryeo** | ~$35 million total consideration | Real-estate media software / content-management platform for photographers and media companies | Strengthen **ShowingTime+**, listing media, interactive floor plans, agent/seller marketing tools |
| Sept. 11, 2023 | **Spruce** | ~$19 million cash, net of cash acquired | Tech-enabled title and escrow platform | Build digital closing capabilities inside the “housing super app” |
| Dec. 8, 2023 | **Follow Up Boss** | ~$399 million cash net of cash acquired + up to $100 million contingent consideration; accounting purchase price ~$484 million | CRM system for agents, teams and brokerages | Add a core agent workflow/SaaS hub; help manage leads from connection to close |
| Oct. 7, 2024 | **Virtual Staging AI / VSAI** | Price not separately disclosed; Zillow’s 2024 cash flow statement shows $7 million cash paid for acquisitions, net | AI virtual-staging technology for listing photos | Add AI-enabled listing media to Zillow Showcase / agent product suite |

A late-2022 precursor, **VRX Media**, also fits the same pattern: real-estate photography, drone imagery, virtual staging, 3D tours and HD images offered through ShowingTime+.

## 2) The strategy: build the tools around the transaction, especially for agents and sellers

The acquisitions were not random. They cluster into three connected themes:

### A. Listing media and seller/agent marketing
Aryeo, VSAI and VRX Media expand Zillow’s ability to help agents and sellers create richer listings—interactive floor plans, photography workflows, virtual staging, and immersive media. This supports Zillow Showcase and ShowingTime+.

### B. Agent productivity and CRM software
Follow Up Boss was the largest deal and the clearest signal that Zillow wants more recurring, software-like revenue from real-estate professionals. Zillow’s 2024 10-K says Follow Up Boss revenue is generated through a SaaS CRM that gives agents, teams and brokerages “a central hub to manage real estate transactions from connection to close.”

### C. Transaction completion / closing infrastructure
Spruce gives Zillow technology and operations for title and escrow, supporting its goal of a more digital transaction from touring to closing, especially alongside Zillow Home Loans.

In short: **Zillow has been buying components of the transaction stack—not inventory.** That is consistent with its post-iBuying model: monetize demand, software, services and transaction attachment rather than owning homes.

## 3) How this aligns with Zillow’s evolving revenue mix

Zillow’s reported revenue mix shows why these deals make sense.

### Revenue mix, 2023 vs. 2024

| Revenue category | 2023 revenue | 2023 mix | 2024 revenue | 2024 mix | Growth |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Residential | $1.452B | 74.65% | $1.594B | 71.29% | 9.78% |
| Mortgages | $96M | 4.94% | $145M | 6.48% | 51.04% |
| **For Sale revenue** | **$1.548B** | **79.59%** | **$1.739B** | **77.77%** | **12.34%** |
| Rentals | $357M | 18.35% | $453M | 20.26% | 26.89% |
| Other | $40M | 2.06% | $44M | 1.97% | 10.00% |
| **Total** | **$1.945B** | **100.00%** | **$2.236B** | **100.00%** | **14.96%** |

### Interpretation

1. **Residential remains the core revenue pool.**  
   Even though its share fell from 74.65% to 71.29%, Residential still represented more than 70% of 2024 revenue. Most acquisitions—Follow Up Boss, Aryeo, VSAI and VRX Media—directly strengthen this category. Zillow explicitly includes Follow Up Boss, ShowingTime+, StreetEasy for-sale products, Premier Agent and new construction inside Residential revenue.

2. **The mix inside Residential is becoming more software-and-services oriented.**  
   Residential is no longer just Premier Agent advertising. Zillow’s definition now includes ShowingTime+, Follow Up Boss, StreetEasy for-sale products and business-technology solutions. The acquisition program is therefore aligned with a shift from pure lead advertising toward **agent SaaS, listing-marketing products, media services and workflow tools**.

3. **For-sale revenue is still dominant, but Zillow is trying to capture more of each transaction.**  
   Residential plus Mortgages—Zillow’s “For Sale revenue”—was $1.739B in 2024, or 77.77% of total revenue. Spruce and Follow Up Boss support a broader path from lead to transaction to closing, while Zillow Home Loans benefits from deeper integration in enhanced markets. Mortgages revenue grew 51.04% in 2024, and Zillow’s loan origination volume more than doubled, which makes transaction integration strategically important.

4. **Rentals is growing faster, but acquisitions were not primarily rental-focused.**  
   Rentals grew 26.89% in 2024 and rose to 20.26% of total revenue. Zillow appears to be scaling rentals more through product, traffic, multifamily listings and partnerships—such as its Realtor.com multifamily listings partnership—rather than acquisitions. So the acquisition program is **less about chasing the fastest-growing reported category** and more about reinforcing Zillow’s largest monetization engine: residential for-sale.

## Bottom line

Zillow’s 2023–2024 acquisition strategy was a **selective, product-led roll-up of capabilities around the residential transaction**: CRM, listing media, virtual staging, title/escrow and agent workflow. This aligns with the company’s evolving revenue mix because Residential/For Sale remains the majority of revenue, while the composition of that revenue is moving beyond Premier Agent lead advertising toward **software, marketplace services, listing enhancement and transaction attachment**. At the same time, Spruce and Follow Up Boss also support Zillow’s longer-term ambition to monetize more of the home-buying funnel, including mortgages and closing services.
